title: Lead Application Development Engineer, Generative AI Products - Senior Vice President
company_focus: Global bank building internal GenAI assistant platform
description:
The Opportunity

This is your chance to build the foundational systems for
'Citi Assist', a Generative AI assistant that will reach
every Citi employee globally. You'll join us on the ground
floor, shaping a high-visibility platform and helping to
define the standards for how a global bank adopts this
technology safely and at scale.

This is a hands-on technical leadership role. You'll spend
your time equally between writing code, architecting
systems, and shaping both the technical and non-technical
direction of our team and product. We're looking for someone
who leads from the front, setting the standard through their
own work while elevating everyone around them.

What You'll Do

Lead, code, and deliver. You'll act as the technical anchor
for our backend services, but this isn't a hands-off role.
You'll lead the design and development of robust, scalable
systems, contribute high-quality code regularly, and set the
standard for the rest of the team through your own work.

Own critical systems. You'll take ownership of core platform
capabilities like the services that run our background jobs
and autonomous agents. Your focus will be on building for
reliability, performance, and operational excellence.

Shape our technical direction. You'll be a key voice in our
technical decisions, helping to define our architecture and
technical roadmap. You'll partner with the product owner and
scrum master to plan our work, ensuring we're building the
right things in the right way.

Champion safety and security. Building safe, sound, and
compliant systems is non-negotiable. You'll lead by example,
thinking about everything from technical security to the
emerging challenges of AI safety.

Be a force multiplier. You'll make the entire team better
through thoughtful guidance, constructive code reviews, and
by unblocking others when they're stuck. Your success is
measured by the success and growth of your team.

Communicate with purpose. You'll foster a culture of clear,
direct, and proactive communication. In a distributed team,
this is essential. You'll model this behaviour, ensuring
information about progress, blockers, and decisions flows
freely.

What We're Looking ForYou should have

- Proven experience leading technical projects and mentoring
  other engineers
- Deep, hands-on experience building, deploying, and
  maintaining production-grade backend services. You're
  still writing code regularly and you're good at it.
- Strong proficiency in at least one backend language (Python,
  Java, Go, or TypeScript/Node.js) with a willingness to
  work across our stack as needed
- Experience architecting and designing scalable, distributed
  systems
- A firm grasp of software design patterns and a commitment to
  writing clean, testable, and maintainable code
- Strong experience with containerised environments (we use
  OpenShift, but Kubernetes experience transfers well)
- Practical experience with CI/CD pipelines and a belief in
  automating things
- Solid experience working with relational databases like
  Postgres

What matters most to us

A hands-on technical leader. You believe that the best way
to lead is by doing. You're equally comfortable in
architecture discussions and pull requests. You don't just
tell people what to build, you show them how.

A pragmatic builder. You take pride in your craft and build
software that lasts. You know how to lead a team to make the
right trade-offs between speed and long-term quality.

A bias for action and a focus on shipping. You understand
our goal is to deliver value to users, not to chase
perfection. You know when to move fast and when to invest in
doing things properly.

A solutions-first mindset. You find the best path to solve
the problem, whether that's writing new code, improving an
old system, or integrating the right tool. The outcome is
what matters.

Strong communication and influence. You can articulate your
technical vision and bring others along with you. You're
comfortable having difficult conversations and making tough
calls when needed.

A collaborative spirit. You thrive in team environments
where you can learn from others and help them grow. You see
mentoring and knowledge-sharing as a core part of your role.

Bonus points for

- Strong Python experience (this is our primary backend
  language)
- Experience with other languages like TypeScript, Go, or Java
- Direct experience with Generative AI concepts, LLMs, or
  related projects
- Experience with data pipelines and ETL tools like Apache
  Airflow or Dagster
- Experience working in highly regulated environments

Our Tech Stack

We work primarily with Python on the backend, though we also
use TypeScript, Go, and Java in places. Our frontend is
built with TypeScript, React, and Next.js. We use Postgres
for our database, run everything on Kubernetes (specifically
Red Hat OpenShift), and our DevOps setup includes GitHub and
CI/CD tools like Tekton and Harness.

Our Team and Mission

We're the Common Platform Engineering team for GenAI, a
central group in the Chief Technology Office. We're a
focused agile team that owns our work from idea to
production.

Our culture is built on a shared belief in engineering
craftsmanship, backed by leadership that actually
understands what that means. We're on a journey to prove
what's possible inside a large organisation, and that means
navigating some legacy while building the future. This isn't
a role for someone who wants everything to be perfect on day
one. It's for a leader who wants to help us get there,
influencing how we solve problems and raising our collective
bar.

We have a dual mission. First, we're building 'Citi Assist',
the GenAI digital assistant for our colleagues. Second,
we're providing the platforms and guidance that enable the
entire organisation to adopt GenAI safely and effectively.

This is not a typical banking job. You'll build foundational
technology that has a direct, positive impact on hundreds of
thousands of people. You'll have the executive support to
tackle big problems and real autonomy to shape our
solutions. Most importantly, you'll join a passionate,
collaborative team where we all learn from each other and
push each other to do our best work.

responsibilities: Lead design and development of backend services for Citi Assist, a GenAI assistant platform reaching all Citi employees globally
Own and build critical platform capabilities including autonomous agents and background job services
Contribute high-quality code regularly while setting engineering standards for the team
Shape technical architecture and roadmap, partnering with product owner and scrum master
Champion safety, security, and compliance in AI systems
Mentor engineers through code reviews, guidance, and knowledge-sharing
Foster clear communication in a distributed team environment
use_cases: Internal GenAI digital assistant (Citi Assist) for all Citi employees globally
Common platform enabling organization-wide safe GenAI adoption
Background job orchestration for AI-powered services
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building and owning core platform
capabilities for 'Citi Assist', a GenAI assistant, including
autonomous agents and background job services. The role is
hands-on with GenAI systems (LLMs, agents) in a platform
engineering capacity, making it ai-first despite being
primarily a backend engineering role.
